definitions.usage_example: I like coffee, but I prefer tea.
definitions.usage_example_es: Me gusta el café, pero prefiero el té.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to introduce a phrase or clause contrasting with what has already been said.
definitions.usage_context_es: Usado para introducir una frase o cláusula que contrasta con lo que ya se ha dicho.

definitions.usage_example: If it rains, I will stay home.
definitions.usage_example_es: Si llueve, me quedaré en casa.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to introduce a condition (something that must happen first).
definitions.usage_context_es: Usado para introducir una condición (algo que debe pasar primero).

definitions.meaning: O si no / De lo contrario
definitions.usage_example: Hurry up, or else we will miss the bus.
definitions.usage_example_es: Date prisa o si no perderemos el bus.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to warn about a consequence.
definitions.usage_context_es: Used to warn about a consequence.

definitions.usage_example: I will go even if it rains.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to show that something will still happen under a condition.
definitions.usage_context_es: Used to show that something will still happen under a condition.

definitions.meaning: En caso de que / Por si
definitions.usage_example: Take an umbrella in case it rains.
definitions.usage_example_es: Lleva un paraguas por si llueve.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to prepare for a possible situation.
definitions.usage_context_es: Used to prepare for a possible situation.
